content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Cornmission
JuIy 25, 1990
Jirn Rogers
city Treasurer
275 East olive Avenue
P. O. Box 714 5
Burbank, cA 91510
Re: Your Request for Advice
our File No. A-90-352
Dear Mr. Rogers:
You have requested reconsideration of the advice which we
provided to you in Advice Letter No. I-89-719. The fol1o$ring
ldvice is based upon your llay 15, 1990 letter, in which you
clarified a discrepancy in the facts, and our telephone conversa-
tions, which added new facts. 
This letter confirns the tentative
advice gj,ven during our telephone conversation on July 6, 1990.
OUESTTONS
You are the Treasurer for the city of Burbank and you have
personal investment interests with the Bank of America, Fj-rst
fnterstate Bank, chase Manhattan Bank and security Pacific
National Bank.
(1) uay you participate in the decision on the RFP (request
for proposals) regarding which bank the city will engage to
transact certain city business?
(2) once the bank has been selected, may you participate in
decisions regarding the city's investments and transactions with
the bank?
. 
CONCLUSIONS
The Act prohibits you from participating in or attenpting to
influence any governmental decisions that wilL have a foreseeable
and naterial financial effect on any of the banks in which you
have personal investments.
(1) You nay not participate in the decision
because of your j,nvestnent of more than $1O,OOO in
America.
on the RFP
Bank of
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958O4-OSOZ . (916)322-5660


File No. A-9 O-3 62
Page 2
(21 once a contract for certain services has been airarded to
a bank, you may transact business and obtain those services from
the bank, even if it is one in which you have an investment inter-
est. 
Under those circumstances, you are not exercising your
discretion regarding those services obtained from that bank. If
you exercise discretion regarding the services you obtain from
that bank, or any other bank, the advice given to you in letter
I-89-719 must be followed.
FACTS
The facts regarding your personal investnents remain the same
as those stated in Advice Letter No. I-89-719. In your position
as the Treasurer for the city of Burbank, you make decisions
reqarding the investment of city funds. In your letter you stated
thit you do not make investments i.n Bank of Anerica, First
fnterstate Bank, Security Pacific National Bank, chase Manhattan
Bank, or in any bank.
The city of Burbank has checking accounts with each of the
above-named institutions. 
The city buys government bonds, govern-
ment agency instruments, banker's acceptances (on foreign banks)
and commercial paper (not on banks) and uses the banks as brokers
for the transactions. The city buys investments through these
banks, but does not make investnents in these banks.
Therefore, you do not purchase stocks, bonds, time certificates of
deposit or any instrument that is an obligation of a bank.
A conmittee, consisting of the Assistant city ltanager,
ltanagement Services Director, conmunity Devel,opment Director, As-
sistint city Treasurer, and yourself, as Treasurer, will be decid-
ing on a RFP (request for proposals). Six banks, including the
Bank of America, have submitted proposals and one !ri1l be selected
as the bank with which the city will transact certain business.
The city budget contains $67,000 which will be paid to the bank as
the annual f ee for i.ts servi.ces.
' 
ANALYSIS
Advice Letter No. I-89-719 gave a thorough analysis of the
nateriality regulations and this letter reconfirms our earlier
advice regarding disqual ification. 
As lras discussed, the Act
prohibits you from part-icipating in or atteupting to influence any
governmental decisions that will have a foreseeable and naterial
financial effect on any of the banks in which you have your
personal investroents.
As we discussed during our telephone conversation on July 6,
1990, you may not participate in the decision on the RFp because
of your investment of nore than glo,ooo in Bank of America.
Pursuant to Regulation 18702.1(a) (2) and (b) (2), the effect of the
RFP decision is roaterial as to Bank of Anerica because it is
directly j.nvolved in the decision.


File No. A-9O-3 62
Page 3
However, once the above-mentioned conmittee has selected and
contracted with a bank for certain services, you may transact
business and obtain those services, even if it is a bank in which
you have an investment interest. 
under those circumstances, you
are not exercising your own discretion regarding the services
obtained from that bank. For example, if the city decides to use
Bank of America as its broker for all purchases of Treasury bonds,
you may carry out your duties as Treasurer and purchase the bonds
fron Bank of Ameri.ca, since you do not appear to have any discre-
tion in the matter.
If you are exercising discretion regarding the services
obtained from any bank, the advice given to you in letter 1-89-7 f9
must be follorred. As explained in that letter, you may not
exercise your discretion to obtain services or transact business
irith a bank in which you have an investment interest if there will
be a foreseeable and naterial financial effect on the bank.
I trust that this letter has ansrrered your questions. If you
have any further guestions regarding this natter, Please contact
ne at (915) 322-59oL.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
